chief_complaint: An 82-year-old woman presented with recurrent ischemic strokes.
history: The patient has atrial fibrillation and was on oral anticoagulation therapy. She was referred for left atrial appendage closure due to recurrent ischemic strokes despite therapy.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) demonstrated slow flow within the left atrial appendage along with a large atrial septal aneurysm (ASA) exhibiting prominent spontaneous echo contrast (SEC).
correct_diagnosis: Combined left atrial appendage and atrial septal aneurysm/patent foramen ovale closure in high-risk atrial fibrillation patients
differentials: Combined left atrial appendage and atrial septal aneurysm/patent foramen ovale closure in high-risk atrial fibrillation patients
Atrial septal aneurysm with patent foramen ovale
Atrial fibrillation with recurrent ischemic strokes
correct_ranking: Combined left atrial appendage and atrial septal aneurysm/patent foramen ovale closure in high-risk atrial fibrillation patients
Atrial fibrillation with recurrent ischemic strokes
Atrial septal aneurysm with patent foramen ovale
